\usepackage{babel}
\begin{document}
Variables

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline 
symbol & description\tabularnewline
\hline 
\hline 
$Y$ & Output\tabularnewline
\hline 
$C$ & Consumption\tabularnewline
\hline 
$MC_{t}$ & Marginal Cost\tabularnewline
\hline 
$N_{t}$ & Labor input\tabularnewline
\hline 
$D_{t}$ & dividend\tabularnewline
\hline 
$W_{t}$ & Wage Rate\tabularnewline
\hline 
$R^{N}_{t}$ & Nominal Interest Rate\tabularnewline
\hline 
$R_{t}$ & Real Interest Rate\tabularnewline
\hline

\hline 
$m_{t}$ & Monetary Shock\tabularnewline
\hline 
$a_{t}$ & Technology Shock\tabularnewline
\hline 
$g_{t}$ & Gov. Exp. Shock\tabularnewline
\hline

Parameters

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline 
Symbol & definition\tabularnewline
\hline 
\hline 
$\alpha$ & factor share\tabularnewline
\hline 
$\beta$ & discount rate\tabularnewline
\hline 
$\gamma$ & inverse of EIS\tabularnewline
\hline 
$\varphi$ & inverse of Frisch elasticity \tabularnewline
\hline 
$\psi$ & elasticity between intermediate goods\tabularnewline
\hline 
$\eta$ & price adjustment parameter\tabularnewline
\hline 
$\sigma$ & rate of subsidy\tabularnewline
\hline 
$\phi$ & Taylor rule parameter\tabularnewline
\hline 
$\rho_{i}$ & persistency parameter\tabularnewline
\hline 
$\kappa$ & Slope of Phillips curve\tabularnewline
\hline 
\end{tabular}

\section{Non-linear equations}

Households
\begin{align*}
1 & =\beta E_{t}\frac{C^{-\gamma}_{t+1}}{C^{-\gamma}_{t}}\frac{R^{N}_{t}}{\Pi_{t+1}}\\
W_{t} & =C^{\gamma}_{t}N^{\varphi}_{t}\\
R_{t} & =E_{t}\frac{R^{N}_{t}}{\Pi_{t+1}}
\end{align*}

Firms
\begin{align*}
\eta\Pi_{t}(\Pi_{t}-1)= & \psi[MC_{t}-(1+\sigma)(1-1/\psi)]\\
 & +\beta\eta E_{t}\frac{C^{-\gamma}_{t+1}}{C^{-\gamma}_{t}}\frac{Y_{t+1}}{Y_{t}}\Pi_{t+1}(\Pi_{t+1}-1)\\
W_{t} & =(1-\alpha)MC_{t}Y_{t}/N_{t}\\
D_{t} & =Y_{t}-\frac{\eta}{2}(\Pi_{t}-1)^{2}Y_{t}-W_{t}N_{t}\\
Y_{t} & =\exp(a_{t})N^{1-\alpha}_{t}
\end{align*}

Taylor Rule
\begin{align*}
R^{N}_{t} & =(1/\beta)\exp(-m_{t})\Pi^{\phi}_{t}
\end{align*}

Aggregation
\[
Y_{t}=C_{t}+G_{t}+\frac{\eta}{2}(\Pi_{t}-1)^{2}Y_{t}
\]


\section{SteadyState}

Let $\Pi=1$, $R^{N}=R=1/\beta$, $MC=(1+\sigma)(1-1/\psi)$, $G_{Y}=G/Y$
is given. 
\begin{align*}
N & =\left[\frac{(1-\alpha)MC}{(1-G_{Y})^{\gamma}}\right]^{\frac{1}{(\gamma-1)(1-\alpha)+\varphi+1}}\\
Y & =\left[\frac{(1-\alpha)MC}{(1-G_{Y})^{\gamma}}\right]^{\frac{1-\alpha}{(\gamma-1)(1-\alpha)+\varphi+1}}\\
W & =(1-\alpha)MC\left[\frac{(1-\alpha)MC}{(1-G_{Y})^{\gamma}}\right]^{\frac{-\alpha}{(\gamma-1)(1-\alpha)+\varphi+1}}\\
C & =(1-G_{Y})Y\\
D & =\alpha Y
\end{align*}
When $\alpha=G_{Y}=0$ and $MC=1$, then $N=Y=W=C=1$.

\section{Log-linear equations}

Households
\begin{align*}
r_{t} & =\gamma E_{t}\Delta c_{t+1}\\
w_{t} & =\gamma c_{t}+\varphi w_{t}\\
r_{t} & =r^{N}_{t}-E_{t}\pi_{t+1}
\end{align*}

Firms
\begin{align*}
\pi_{t} & =(\varphi/\eta)MCmc_{t}+\beta E_{t}\pi_{t+1}\\
w_{t} & =mc_{t}+y_{t}-n_{t}\\
d_{t} & =(1-MC)y_{t}-(1-\alpha)MC\\
y_{t} & =a_{t}+(1-\alpha)n_{t}
\end{align*}

Taylor Rule
\[
r^{N}_{t}=\phi\pi_{t}-m_{t}
\]

Aggregation 
\[
y_{t}=(1-G_{Y})c_{t}+g_{t}
\]


\section{Solution}

Marginal cost
\begin{align*}
mc_{t} & =w_{t}+n_{t}-y_{t}\\
 & =\gamma c_{t}+(\varphi+1)n_{t}-y_{t}\\
 & =\gamma\frac{y_{t}-g_{t}}{1-G_{Y}}+(\varphi+1)\frac{y_{t}-a_{t}}{1-\alpha}-y_{t}\\
 & =(\tilde{\gamma}+\tilde{\varphi})(y_{t}-\zeta_{a}a_{t}-\zeta_{g}g_{t})
\end{align*}
where 
\begin{align*}
\zeta_{a}=\frac{\tilde{\varphi}+1}{\tilde{\varphi}+\tilde{\gamma}}, & \zeta_{g}=\frac{\tilde{\gamma}}{\tilde{\varphi}+\tilde{\gamma}}\\
\tilde{\varphi}=\frac{\varphi+\alpha}{1-\alpha}, & \tilde{\gamma}=\frac{\gamma}{1-G_{Y}}
\end{align*}

Three NK equations
\begin{align*}
r_{t} & =r^{f}_{t}+\tilde{\gamma}E_{t}[\Delta x_{t+1}-\Delta g_{t+1}]\\
\pi_{t} & =\kappa x_{t}+\beta E_{t}\pi_{t+1}\\
r_{t} & =\phi\pi_{t}-E_{t}[\pi_{t+1}]-m_{t}
\end{align*}
where 

\begin{align*}
x_{t} & =y_{t}-y^{f}_{t}\\
y^{f}_{t} & =\zeta_{a}a_{t}+\zeta_{g}g_{t}\\
r^{f}_{t} & =\tilde{\gamma}E_{t}\Delta y^{f}_{t+1}=-\tilde{\gamma}[(1-\rho_{a})\zeta_{a}a_{t}+(1-\rho_{g})\zeta_{g}g_{t}]\\
\kappa & =MC(\tilde{\varphi}+\tilde{\gamma})(\psi/\eta)
\end{align*}

Only monetary shock ($a_{t}=g_{t}=0$): Since $E_{t}\pi_{t+1}=\rho_{m}\pi_{t}$
and $E_{t}x_{t+1}=\rho_{m}x_{t}$, we obtain
\begin{align*}
r_{t} & =\tilde{\gamma}(\rho_{m}-1)x_{t}\\
\pi_{t} & =\kappa_{m}x_{t}\\
r_{t} & =(\phi-\rho_{m})\pi_{t}-m_{t}
\end{align*}
where $r^{f}_{t}=0$, $x_{t}=y_{t}$, and $\kappa_{m}=\kappa/(1-\beta\rho_{m})$.
AD-AS model
\begin{align*}
\pi_{t} & =\frac{\tilde{\gamma}(\rho_{m}-1)x_{t}+m_{t}}{\phi-\rho_{m}}\\
\pi_{t} & =\kappa_{m}x_{t}.
\end{align*}
Thus, $x_{t}=\Omega m_{t}$, $\pi_{t}=\kappa_{m}\Omega m_{t}$, and
$r_{t}=-\Omega_{m}m_{t}$ where 
\begin{align*}
\Omega & =\frac{1}{\kappa_{m}(\phi-\rho_{m})+\tilde{\gamma}(1-\rho_{m})}\\
\Omega_{m} & =\frac{\tilde{\gamma}(1-\rho_{m})}{\kappa_{m}(\phi-\rho_{m})+\tilde{\gamma}(1-\rho_{m})},\;0<\Omega,\;\Omega_{m}<1.
\end{align*}

Only technology shock ($m_{t}=g_{t}=0$):
\begin{align*}
r_{t} & =r^{f}_{t}+\tilde{\gamma}(\rho_{a}-1)x_{t}\\
\pi_{t} & =\kappa_{a}x_{t}\\
r_{t} & =(\phi-\rho_{a})\pi_{t}
\end{align*}
where $r^{f}_{t}=\tilde{\gamma_{a}}(\rho_{a}-1)a_{t}$, $x_{t}=y_{t}-\zeta_{a}a_{t}$,
and $\kappa_{a}=\kappa/(1-\beta\rho_{a})$. AD-AS model:
\begin{align*}
\pi_{t} & =\frac{\tilde{\gamma}(\rho_{a}-1)x_{t}+\tilde{\gamma_{a}}\zeta_{a}(\rho_{a}-1)a_{t}}{\phi-\rho_{a}}\\
\pi_{t} & =\kappa_{a}x_{t}.
\end{align*}
Thus, $x_{t}=-\zeta_{a}\Omega_{a}a_{t}$ $\pi_{t}=-\kappa_{a}\zeta_{a}\Omega_{a}a_{t}$,
and $r_{t}=-(\phi-\rho_{a})\kappa_{a}\zeta_{a}\Omega_{a}a_{t}$, where
\begin{align*}
\Omega_{a} & =\frac{\tilde{\gamma}(1-\rho_{a})}{\kappa_{a}(\phi-\rho_{m})+\tilde{\gamma}(1-\rho_{a})},0<\Omega_{a}<1.
\end{align*}

Only government expenditure shock ($m_{t}=a_{t}=0$):
\begin{align*}
r_{t} & =r^{f}_{t}+\tilde{\gamma}(\rho_{g}-1)(x_{t}-g_{t})\\
\pi_{t} & =\kappa_{g}x_{t}\\
r_{t} & =(\phi-\rho_{g})\pi_{t}
\end{align*}
where $r^{f}_{t}=\tilde{\gamma_{g}}(\rho_{g}-1)g_{t}$, $x_{t}=y_{t}-\zeta_{g}g_{t}$,
and $\kappa_{g}=\kappa/(1-\beta\rho_{g})$. AD-AS model:
\begin{align*}
\pi_{t} & =\frac{\tilde{\gamma}(\rho_{g}-1)x_{t}+\tilde{\gamma_{g}}(1-\zeta_{g})(\rho_{g}-1)g_{t}}{\phi-\rho_{g}}\\
\pi_{t} & =\kappa_{g}x_{t}.
\end{align*}
Thus, $x_{t}=(1-\zeta_{g})\Omega_{g}g_{t}$, $\pi_{t}=\kappa_{g}(1-\zeta_{g})\Omega_{g}g_{t}$,
$r_{t}=(\phi-\rho_{g})\kappa_{g}(1-\zeta_{g})\Omega_{g}g_{t}$ where
\begin{align*}
\Omega_{g} & =\frac{\tilde{\gamma}(1-\rho_{g})}{\kappa_{a}(\phi-\rho_{m})+\tilde{\gamma}(1-\rho_{a})},\;0<\Omega_{g}<1.
\end{align*}

In sum 
\begin{align*}
x_{t} & =\Omega m_{t}-\zeta_{a}\Omega_{a}a_{t}+(1-\zeta_{g})\Omega_{g}g_{t}\\
\pi_{t} & =\kappa_{m}\Omega m_{t}-\kappa_{a}\zeta_{a}\Omega_{a}a_{t}+\kappa_{g}(1-\zeta_{g})\Omega_{g}g_{t}\\
r_{t} & =-\Omega_{m}m_{t}-(\phi-\rho_{a})\kappa_{a}\zeta_{a}\Omega_{a}a_{t}+(\phi-\rho_{g})\kappa_{g}(1-\zeta_{g})\Omega_{g}g_{t}
\end{align*}
Note:
\begin{align*}
y_{t} & =x_{t}+y^{f}_{t}=\Omega_{m}m_{t}+\zeta_{a}(1-\Omega_{a})a_{t}+(\zeta_{g}+(1-\zeta_{g})\Omega_{g})g_{t}\\
(1-G_{Y})c_{t} & =y_{t}-g_{t}=\Omega_{m}m_{t}+\zeta_{a}(1-\Omega_{a})a_{t}-(1-\zeta_{g})(1-\Omega_{g})g_{t}\\
(1-\alpha)n_{t} & =y_{t}-a_{t}=\Omega_{m}m_{t}-\{1-\zeta_{a}(1-\Omega_{a})\}a_{t}-(1-\zeta_{g})(1-\Omega_{g})g_{t}.
\end{align*}

\end{document}
